'''Dane Dorahy''' ({{birth date|1977|12|17|df=y}}) is an Australian [[rugby league]] coach and former professional rugby league footballer.

He played as a {{rlp|fb|fe}}, or {{rlp|hb}} in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at club level for [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Western Suburbs]] ([[List of Western Suburbs Magpies players|Heritage No. 1052]]) in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] ([[List of Wakefield Trinity players|Heritage No. 1165]]) and [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] ([[List of Halifax R.L.F.C. players|Heritage No. 1172]]) in [[Super League]], and the [[Rochdale Hornets]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] ([[List of Hull Kingston Rovers players|Heritage No. 1013]]) in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

== Background ==
Dorahy is the son of former international rugby league player and coach, [[John Dorahy]].<ref name="Statistics at loverugbyleague.com" /><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org" />

== Coaching career ==
Dorahy took up a coaching role with the [[Wests Tigers]] in 2007, where he spent two seasons, before moving to the [[Illawarra Cutters]].<ref name="TRL Castleford Appointment">{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=10 August 2023 |title=Dane Dorahy – who is Castleford Tigers' new assistant coach? |url=https://www.totalrl.com/dane-dorahy-who-is-castleford-tigers-new-assistant-coach/ |access-date=21 November 2023 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> In 2021, he took the head coach role for [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]]' reserve grade, where he took them from last position, to just missing out on Top 5.
2023 Dane became assistant coach / mentor for [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]]' under-21s.<ref name="TRL Castleford Appointment" />

On 9 August 2023, the [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the appointment of Dorahy as an assistant coach on a short-term basis until the end of the season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=9 August 2023 |title=Tigers appoint coaching duo |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8721 |access-date=9 August 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=9 August 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers: Danny Ward unveiled as head coach until end of season following Andy Last's departure |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12936796/castleford-tigers-danny-ward-unveiled-as-head-coach-until-end-of-season-following-andy-lasts-departure |access-date=9 August 2023 |publisher=Sky Sports}}</ref> He joined the Tigers alongside new head coach [[Danny Ward (rugby league)|Danny Ward]] following the departure of [[Andy Last]], and would work with existing coaches [[Craig Lingard]] and [[Scott Murrell]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=9 August 2023 |title=Danny Ward: Castleford Tigers appoint former London coach until end of season |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66455512 |access-date=9 August 2023 |publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref> With six games remaining and Castleford level on points at the bottom of the table, the new coaching staff were tasked with securing [[Super League]] status.<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=9 August 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers appoint Danny Ward and bring in new assistant for Super League survival mission |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-appoint-danny-ward-and-bring-in-new-assistant-for-super-league-survival-mission-4249782 |access-date=9 August 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=9 August 2023 |title=Danny Ward's rallying call to Castleford Tigers fans with survival the goal |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/danny-wards-rallying-call-castleford-27490760 |access-date=9 August 2023 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref> Key victories against [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] and [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] saw the Tigers narrowly survive,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Newsum |first=Matt |date=15 September 2023 |title=Wakefield Trinity relegated after 24 years in Super League following Leigh loss |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66797544 |access-date=21 November 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> and Dorahy departed following the conclusion of the season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=17 October 2023 |title=Thank you to the departing staff members |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8792 |access-date=21 November 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=8 November 2023 |title=Ex-Castleford Tigers assistant coach Dane Dorahy keen on Super League or Championship move |url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-castleford-tigers-assistant-coach-dane-dorahy-keen-on-super-league-or-championship-move/ |access-date=21 November 2023 |website=TotalRL}}</ref>
